title: The Ten Virgins
subtitle: At midnight the cry goes up: Behold, the Bridegroom comes

stillness: Be still, for you do not know the hour.
The Lord longs to find you ready when He comes.
Watch, then, in the quiet — for we know not when the Bridegroom will come.
gospel: The kingdom of heaven shall be like ten virgins,
who took their lamps and went out to meet the Bridegroom and the bride.
Five of them were foolish, and five were wise.
The five foolish, taking their lamps, did not take oil with them;
but the wise took oil in their vessels with their lamps.
And while the Bridegroom tarried, they all slumbered and slept.
But at midnight a cry was made:
Behold, the Bridegroom comes; go out to meet Him.
behold: Attend now to each thing, as if you were present.
Behold the ten virgins going out, lamps in hand, to meet the Bridegroom.
Look closely: five carry oil hidden in their vessels — joy within the conscience.
And five carry lamps that shine, but their vessels are empty.
These are virgins in their deeds, but corrupted in intention.
They do good, and are seen as good by men — yet within they are void of goodness, and evil.
Now the Bridegroom delays; one by one their eyes grow heavy.
They slumber in the weariness of the body; they sleep the sleep of death.
Then, at midnight — suddenly, when none knows or hopes —
the cry of the trumpeting Angels breaks open the dark.
consider: It is great folly to undertake hard and bitter labors, and bear them, without fruit.
Great folly to fast, to keep vigil, to afflict the flesh, to pray —
and to look for no other wages than the empty praise of men.
Of such the Lord says: They have received their reward.
The foolish served the vanity of the five senses;
they ordered their good works not to their due end, but to their own glory.
The wise served the truth for things eternal,
and referred their whole intention to God, whom alone they desire to please.
This oil is the gladness of good works, laid up in the heart and conscience;
for it is called oil because it nourishes and feeds the fire of charity.
See, then, that you do the good work well, and with right intention —
for not to do good, but to do it well, is what is praised.
And when the consummation of the age delays, not only sinners,
but the just and the spiritual are dragged down to negligence.
O how great that cry shall be!
No soul so high in heaven, nor so deep in hell, no body in the tomb,
but it shall hear that cry, and tremble.
prayer: grant me, with the wise virgins, to do good works with right intention,
and watchfully to persevere in them,
that, found ready, I may enter with gladness into Your wedding feast.
I ask, O Lord, that You make known to me my end before it comes,
and do not let me be finished before You have forgiven my sins,
that in the hour of my passing the divine vengeance may not fall upon me,
and the power of darkness may not meet me, nor harm me;
but let the day of examination give me back such as the font of regeneration once made me. Amen.

latinJewel.latin: O quantus clamor, quia non erit anima ita alte in cœlo, nec ita profunde in inferno, nec corpus in sepulchro, quin audiat hunc clamorem, et contremiscat!
latinJewel.english: O how great that cry shall be! For no soul will be so high in heaven, nor so deep in hell, nor any body in the grave, but it will hear this cry, and tremble!
